title: Approach to Long Term Investing from Mutual Fund Perspective | Sailesh Bhan | Nippon | 9th VIPS
description:
In this deep‑dive conversation, we explore how to think about investing using first principles, manage risk intelligently, and navigate volatile markets without getting trapped by noise, narratives, or short‑term emotions.

From AI‑driven investment flows to expectations investing, sector allocation, stock selection, and valuation discipline—this session focuses on how real returns are actually made and what investors often overlook.

Whether you’re a long‑term investor, portfolio manager, or someone trying to build conviction in uncertain markets, this video offers a clear, practical framework you can apply immediately.

About the speaker:
Sailesh Raj Bhan is the CIO Equities at Nippon Life India Asset Management overlooking close to S30bn of Active Equities. Sailesh has over 29 years of experience in Indian Equity Markets with over 21 years at Nippon Life India Asset Management Limited. 

Sailesh is among one of the longest serving equity fund managers in Indian Mutual Fund Industry with over 2 decades of fund management expertise. About one in three folios in Indian Equity Mutual Fund industry is with Nippon Life India Asset Management Ltd. 

He manages the flagship diversified equity schemes like Nippon India Multi Cap Fund since its inception in March 2005 (over 19 years) and Nippon India Large Cap Fund since its inception 2007 (over 17 years).Sailesh also manages the Largest Pharma Sector Fund in India since its inception in May 2004.

Satyadeep Jain is a Director in the Institutional Equities Research team at Ambit, where he leads coverage on the Metals, Mining, Cement, Utilities, and New Energy sectors. With over a decade of experience in equity research across global and Indian markets, he brings deep domain expertise and a structured fundamental approach to evaluating long-term sectoral trends, policy shifts, cost cycles, capex dynamics, and company-level strategy. 

Prior to joining Ambit, Satyadeep worked with leading global research platforms, including Vertical Research Partners, Sterne Agee & Leach, and earlier in his career with Jefferies, where he focused extensively on industrials and commodities across emerging and developed markets. At Ambit, he is responsible for driving sector-focused research, publishing sector insights, and engaging closely with institutional investors, corporate leadership teams, and industry stakeholders. Satyadeep holds an MBA in Finance and Accounting from the NYU Stern School of Business and is also a Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA). 

📢 Presented by: CFA Society India & CFA Institute
🔔 Subscribe for more expert insights from global investment leaders.

1:20 - Background 
5:29 - Introduction
7:09 - Investing flows & AI
9:13 - Navigating markets - simple, but not easy
10:57 - Managing by first principle
18:12 - Overcoming Volatility
27:39 - Where are you making money from? Stock selection vs Sector allocation
32:25 - Managing risk by first principle
38:24 - Right risk investing
41:28 - How to do it practically?
44:29 - Expectations Investing - Narratives vs Realities
48:00 - Do valuations matter?
49:03 - Nothing comes free

As one of the over 160 CFA Institute member societies, the IAIP connects local CFA Institute members to a global network of investment professionals. For the continuing up-gradation of knowledge and skills of members, IAIP conducts speaker events, workshops led by leading finance professionals.

title: Balancing Trust & Growth: The CEO Playbook for Private Banking | 10th IWMC | Mumbai
description:
In private banking, success is built on a delicate balance—earning client trust while delivering consistent business growth.

In this panel discussion from the India Wealth Management Conference, senior leaders share practical perspectives on how the private wealth industry is evolving across India and global markets.

Speakers include:
•        Atul Singh, CEO & MD, LGT Wealth India 
•        Vipul Kapur, Head of Mashreq Private Banking, Mashreq 
•        Apurva Sahijwani, MD & CEO, Avendus Wealth Management (Moderator) 

The discussion moves beyond theory and focuses on real operating challenges in private banking, including client expectations, talent retention, advisory models, and international expansion opportunities.

Key themes covered include:
• Evolution of private wealth management in India vs global markets
• Future direction of private banking business models
• Key challenges in managing HNI/UHNI clients
• Advisory frameworks for family offices
• Growth opportunities in international markets (e.g., UAE)
• Balancing client outcomes, talent retention, and shareholder expectations
• Talent strategy and retention in wealth businesses

This session is highly relevant for wealth managers, private bankers, family office professionals, and investment advisors looking to understand how leading firms are navigating growth while maintaining client trust.

0:00 – Welcome and session introduction
3:20 – Overview of private wealth management in India and globally
5:20 – Future direction of the private wealth business
11:44 – Key challenges in managing private wealth clients
19:48 – Advisory models for family offices
24:34 – Opportunities in the UAE market
28:00 – Balancing clients, talent, and shareholders
35:40 – Talent retention in private wealth businesses
44:00 – Closing remarks
__________________________________________________________________________________________
The Indian Association of Investment Professionals (IAIP), the India Society of CFA Institute, was established in Apr 2005 as an association of local investment professionals consisting of portfolio managers, security analysts, investment advisors and other financial professionals.

As one of the over 160 CFA Institute member societies, the IAIP connects local CFA Institute members to a global network of investment professionals. For the continuing up-gradation of knowledge and skills of members, IAIP conducts speaker events, workshops led by leading finance professionals.
